response.justification: Wei Chen is an accomplished full-stack developer with strong software craftsmanship and distributed-systems experience, but this role requires deep modern C++ expertise and industrial automation domain knowledge. His C++ experience is limited and dated (2012–2015), and his career has focused on web/backend technologies. He lacks required credentials: no formal engineering degree (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech), no industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443/SDL) background, and no demonstrated exposure to industrial protocols, IoT systems, or automation machinery. This is a specialization mismatch despite his overall technical competence.
strengths: 18+ years of software development experience across multiple domains and technologies, with strong problem-solving abilities
Deep expertise in distributed systems, microservices, and event-driven architectures demonstrated at Dunnhumby and Optilyz
Strong commitment to software craftsmanship, TDD, and code quality with proven mentoring and teaching capability
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (2012–2015 at National Instruments/Altom); current career focused on TypeScript/Node.js/web tech, not systems programming
No formal engineering degree (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech); education is in Computer Science and Fine Arts, not engineering disciplines
Zero demonstrated knowledge of IEC 62443, SDL, industrial cybersecurity, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA), or automation machinery domain

key_factors.factor: Industrial cybersecurity and compliance certification (IEC 62443, SDL)
